title: Doncic says he's in custody battle for daughters
content: Lakers star Luka Dončić has separated from his fiancée, Anamaria Goltes, and is in a custody battle over their two daughters, he told ESPN.

title: Sources: Jets get their QB, trade for Geno Smith
content: The Jets are trading for the Raiders' Geno Smith, reuniting the quarterback with the team that drafted him in 2013, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Tuesday.

title: Eagles strike, add CB Woolen on one-year deal
content: Free agent cornerback Riq Woolen reached agreement Tuesday on a one-year deal worth up to $15 million with the Eagles, agent Jason Chayut told ESPN.

title: 76ers' Maxey (finger tendon) out at least 3 weeks
content: 76ers star guard Tyrese Maxey has been diagnosed with a tendon injury to his right pinkie finger and will be re-evaluated in approximately three weeks, the team announced Tuesday.

title: Sources: Pats land Packers' Doubs on 4-year deal
content: The Patriots have agreed to sign former Packers wide receiver Romeo Doubs to a four-year deal worth up to $80 million, sources told ESPN.

title: WAC may bench No. 1 seed Utah Valley over $1M
content: The WAC has threatened to leave Utah Valley out of the men's and women's basketball tournaments over a $1 million payment it says the school hasn't made. The UVU men's team is the top seed in the conference.

title: Kelce says Swift inspired return for 14th season
content: Travis Kelce said that fiancée Taylor Swift was a significant influence on his decision to return to the Chiefs for his 14th NFL season.

title: Arozarena unleashes Raleigh rant over WBC snub
content: Randy Arozarena had vulgar words for Mariners teammate Cal Raleigh after he was left hanging at the World Baseball Classic.

title: 👀 Ultimate what-if: See our NBA alternate reality
content: ESPN analyst Zach Kram examines a reality in which Durant's foot didn't touch the 3-point line in the 2021 Eastern Conference semifinals -- and its ripple effects throughout the NBA.

title: Barnwell: Sorting out the winners, losers and dominoes from free agency's wild opening day
content: Tua Tagovailoa in a great spot? No real help for C.J. Stroud? Here's the best and worst of free agency.

title: Bubble Watch: Santa Clara could very well give the WCC three bids
content: The Broncos could go toe-to-toe with the rest of the bubble, but beating Saint Mary's may give them an edge.

title: How the schedule could impact three key NBA playoff races
content: With so many teams tanking, some playoff-bound teams could capitalize on easy schedules down the stretch.

title: Six wins away: How UConn is closing in on 13th NCAA title, seventh perfect season
content: Even after Paige Bueckers departed for the WNBA, the Huskies kept rolling and are closing in on a repeat.

title: Atlético Madrid put one foot in Champions League quarterfinals as Spurs implode
content: Not even the Metropolitano faithful knew what to make of Atlético's stunning win, with Spurs making numerous critical errors.

title: 'Man, just please come back safe': What it's like for an MLB team when its stars are in the WBC
content: While its top players are out taking on the world, we checked in on life back at home during spring training.

title: From college QB to Team USA starter in the WBC: The incredible rise of Nolan McLean
content: Eight starts into his MLB career, the Mets' budding ace is making himself at home in the U.S. rotation.

title: Women's Bracketology: How did the Power 5 finals affect the bracket?
content: UConn dominated, Texas locked up the No. 3 overall seed, Duke moved to a No. 2 and West Virginia should host NCAA tournament games.

title: Champ Week takeaways: Should UConn or UCLA be No. 1 overall?
content: A week before Selection Sunday, which teams should be worried (Tennessee) or encouraged (Notre Dame)?

title: What to expect from Rosen, Jiricek, other top NHL prospects traded at the deadline
content: How will those players impact their new teams? When will they play meaningful minutes at the NHL level? Here are scouting reports on 10 big names.

title: Latest mini NFL mock draft: Yates projects picks 1-10 based on the free agency frenzy
content: How has NFL free agency affected 2026 draft plans? Here are Field Yates' updated predictions for picks Nos. 1-10 after the first wave of deals.

title: Sources: TE Knox stays with Bills on new deal
content: The Bills found a way to keep tight end Dawson Knox on the 2026 roster amid a difficult cap situation by signing him to a new three-year contract, sources told ESPN's Jeremy Fowler on Tuesday.

title: Texans bolster O-line, reach deal with OT Smith
content: Free agent offensive tackle Braden Smith reached agreement with the Houston Texans on a two-year, $25 million contract that includes $13.5 million fully guaranteed, his agent told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Giants address CB need, agree to sign Newsome
content: The Giants addressed their need at cornerback, agreeing with Greg Newsome II on a one-year deal worth up to $10 million, his agents told ESPN.

title: Source: WR Raymond leaves Lions to join Bears
content: Kalif Raymond has agreed to sign a one-year contract with the Bears, reuniting the wide receiver with Ben Johnson, a source told ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.

title: Source: LB Lloyd gets $45M deal with Panthers
content: Former Jaguars linebacker Devin Lloyd agreed to a three-year, $45 million deal with the Panthers, a source told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Monday.

title: Sources: Tua to sign 1-year deal with Falcons
content: Former Miami Dolphins quarterback Tua Tagovailoa plans to sign a one-year deal with the Atlanta Falcons, a source told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Commanders land Oweh on 4-year, $100M deal
content: Former Chargers defensive end Odafe Oweh, who has 17.5 sacks since 2024, has agreed with the Commanders on a four-year, $100 million deal, his agents told ESPN.

title: Sources: QB Willis, Fins reach 3-year, $67.5M deal
content: Free agent QB Malik Willis, after reviving his career with the Packers, has agreed to a three-year, $67.5 million deal with the Dolphins, sources told ESPN.

title: NWSL ambition rankings: Which teams are raising standards, and which aren't trying enough?
content: The NWSL has teams that are building stadiums, signing stars and spending money. Meanwhile, other teams, well, aren't doing any of that.

title: Is 23 red cards in Brazilian mass brawl a world record? Not even close
content: A lot of red cards were handed out in Cruzeiro's win over Atlético-MG, but it's nowhere near the world record.

title: On the rise? Teams, players poised to improve in 2026
content: From potential CFP contenders to bounce-back candidates, we've got good feelings about these programs.

title: WNBA CBA negotiations: What if a deal isn't reached by Tuesday's 'deadline'?
content: The WNBA and the union exchanged proposals over the weekend, but there's no sign a deal is imminent. How far apart are both sides?

title: Guide to NFL offseason signings: Can Geno Smith rebound from 2025 in New York?
content: Your one-stop shop for fantasy football reaction to all the key player movement via free agency and trades.

title: Eric Karabell's 'Do not draft' list
content: Eric Karabell identifies the players unlikely to be on his roster this season due to too much risk, too much hype or not enough at-bats.

title: News or noise: Reds ace Hunter Greene shelved for months
content: Eric Karabell keeps tabs on the baseball world and tells you what is relevant to fantasy baseball managers.

title: 2026 Players Championship: Ranking the top 25 players in the field
content: We run through the top contenders among the 123-man field to win the PGA Tour's flagship event at TPC Sawgrass.

title: Boxing divisional rankings: Jai Opetaia keeps top spot and claims new title
content: Jai Opetaia beat Brandon Glanton to keep his No. 1 spot at cruiserweight. Here are the latest ESPN divisional rankings.

title: Zuckerberg acquires Moltbook that rival Sam Altman made ‘fun of’ by calling it …
content: Meta has acquired Moltbook, a platform where AI bots discuss their creators, a move that brings co-founders Matt Schlicht and Ben Parr to Meta's AI research division. This acquisition highlights the intensifying competition for AI talent and technology as autonomous agents gain broader industry relevance. The deal's financial terms remain undisclosed.

title: India considering naval escort for ships in Strait of Hormuz amid Iran war: Report
content: India is contemplating deploying naval ships to escort its merchant vessels through the Strait of Hormuz amidst escalating Middle East tensions. This consideration follows urgent pleas from Indian shipowners seeking protection for their commercial fleets navigating the crucial, now high-risk, waterway.

title: Anthropic takes US govt to court; CFO says: After President Trump’s social media posts, a major ...
content: Anthropic CEO apologized for a memo after talks with the Pentagon broke down, only to file a lawsuit against the Trump administration days later. The company seeks to overturn a national security threat designation, claiming it jeopardizes billions in revenue and irreparably harms its business and investor confidence.

title: Amazon sends complaint letter to FCC against Elon Musk's Starlink
content: Amazon's Project Kuiper has urged the FCC to reject SpaceX's proposal for a million-satellite constellation, citing a lack of essential technical and space safety details. Amazon argues the plan is speculative, would take centuries to deploy, and could monopolize orbital resources, hindering future space endeavors.

title: Dallas Cowboys eye trade with Pittsburgh Steelers for veteran linebacker
content: The Dallas Cowboys are reportedly exploring trade options with the Pittsburgh Steelers as they search for help at linebacker. After missing several free agent targets, Dallas is considering a deal for a two-time Pro Bowl defender entering the final year of his contract to strengthen its defense.

title: Tennessee Titans linked to $67M San Francisco 49ers wide receiver trade
content: Jauan Jennings faces an uncertain future with the San Francisco 49ers after Tampa Bay Buccaneers legendary wide receiver Mike Evans was brought in. Jennings is a Tennessee native. He is now a coveted free agent. The Tennessee Titans are considered a strong possibility for him. He could provide veteran leadership for second-year quarterback Cam Ward on the gridiron. Jennings has accumulated significant yards and touchdowns during his time with the Niners.

title: Did Candace Owens discontinue ‘Bride of Charlie’ series amid falling views?
content: Candace Owens sparked online debate after appearing to shift focus away from her controversial series about Erika Kirk. A viral social media post claimed the series was sidelined as audience interest declined. Owens instead promoted a new episode discussing geopolitical issues, leaving followers questioning whether the Erika Kirk storyline has ended or simply paused.

title: Anubhav Sinha reacts to Taapsee Pannu’s Bollywood vs South cinema remark
content: Filmmaker Anubhav Sinha recently addressed actor Taapsee Pannu’s remark about the portrayal of women in films. Speaking to Siddharth Kannan in an interview, the director shared his perspective on the debate around body representation in Bollywood and South Indian cinema. He also spoke about working with Taapsee and his past comment comparing her to Kangana Ranaut.

title: Fuel crisis: Major UAE oil refinery shut; global energy body calls 'emergency meeting'
content: A major UAE oil refinery halted operations following a drone attack, intensifying fears of a global energy crisis.  The International Energy Agency is convening an emergency meeting to assess supply risks and consider releasing strategic oil reserves.  Escalating attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure are causing severe disruptions across various industries, prompting calls for market stabilization.

title: 'This isn’t right': Vaughan fumes as SA, Windies remain stuck in India
content: After their T20 World Cup 2026 exit, West Indies and South Africa remained stranded in Kolkata for over a week, drawing criticism from Michael Vaughan, who questioned why England flew home within 36 hours of elimination. Officials said the delay was due to Middle East airspace restrictions affecting routes to the Caribbean and South Africa, forcing cancellations and charter flight complications.

title: Why ICC penalised Arshdeep Singh: Rules, sanctions, punishment - All you need to know
content: India pacer Arshdeep Singh has been fined 15% of his match fee and received one demerit point for aggressively throwing the ball at Daryl Mitchell during the T20 World Cup final. This action breached ICC Code of Conduct Article 2.9, relating to inappropriate or dangerous ball throwing. Arshdeep accepted the sanction, marking his first offense within a 24-month period.

title: The Iran war’s hidden crisis: Why water could matter more than oil
content: Amidst the Iran war's focus on oil, a critical vulnerability, water, has been overlooked. Gulf nations heavily depend on desalination plants, now facing damage from missile and drone strikes. The article highlights the potential for a severe water crisis, with international law prohibiting attacks on such vital infrastructure.

title: D.K. Shivakumar hosts dinner to mark six years as Karnataka Congress chief
content: Congratulating Shivakumar on completing six years at the helm of KPCC, Chief Minister Siddaramaiah said his deputy’s sagacity, ideological commitment and hard work is a model for young leaders

title: West Asia conflict: Jaishankar holds ‘detailed’ conversation with Iranian counterpart Araghchi
content: It is not immediately known whether the sinking of an Iranian warship by the U.S. near Sri Lanka on March 4 figured in the conversation between the two leaders

title: India slams Pakistan’s airstrikes on Afghanistan during Ramzan
content: India's Permanent Representative to the UN, Harish Parvathaneni reiterated New Delhi's support for the UN Secretary-General's call for compliance with international law, including international humanitarian law, and for ensuring protection of civilians

title: Yelagiri Hills witness forest fire, two hectares of forest land destroyed
content: Following the incident, special teams have been formed for regular patrolling of vulnerable spots in the hills to prevent such accidents during summer

title: Women’s reservation in Parliament, State assemblies should be implemented: VIT Chancellor
content: The Chancellor said that inequality among women is not restricted to political representations but also in other areas like education and health care

title: Work to construct ₹65.98 crore ROB near Ambur commences
content: Funded under the Tamil Nadu Deposit Fund 2025-26, the two-lane bridge will be 600 metres long and 7.5 metres wide with at least 50 LED streetlights on the bridge and its service lanes

title: K.K. Venugopal biography an attempt to come to terms with himself: N. Ram
content: Says the former Attorney General talks about himself in the book as to how he was an “accidental lawyer”.

title: Odisha hotel and restaurant association flags LPG cylinder shortage issue
content: The hotel and restaurant industry provides livelihood to thousands of employees and supports numerous allied sectors including food supply chains, transport, and local vendors while this disruption will have wider ramification on livelihood, says the association

title: Terrorist killed as Army foils infiltration bid in J&K’s Nowshera
content: Movement of two terrorists was observed in Jhangar area at around 3 p.m. on Tuesday, says Army; one Pakistan-sponsored terrorist was eliminated, and troops have been reoriented to search for the second terrorist, it adds

title: Experts deliberate on North East security at seminar hosted by Assam Rifles
content: The seminar was held at the Major General Samir Sinha Auditorium at USI and brought together senior military officers, security experts, academics and media representatives to deliberate on the evolving security landscape in the region

title: Union Cabinet approves international status for Madurai airport
content: The new status is expected to allow more regular international routes and easier approvals for foreign airlines

title: Exoneration in departmental inquiry will not automatically eclipse criminal prosecution in every bribery case: Karnataka High Court
content: In essence, departmental exoneration may influence, but does not automatically extinguish, criminal prosecution.

title: SC to pronounce verdict in family’s plea to withdraw life support to man in ‘permanent vegetative’ state
content: A Bench of Justices J.B. Pardiwala and K.V. Viswanathan is scheduled to deliver the verdict on the case, which was reserved for final decision on February 15 this year, tomorrow (March 11)

title: Tamil Nadu State Planning Commission calls for legal protection and social security for gig workers
content: In a study, ‘Status of Platform Based Gig Workers in Tamil Nadu,’ the Commission highlights the rapid expansion of platform-based work and calls for targeted interventions to ensure welfare, protection, and long-term sustainability for these workers

title: Committed to protecting the Aravalli hills, says  Bhupender Yadav
content: Minister said that the government is considering the setting up of a High Powered Committee on the matter, as directed by the Supreme Court

title: Trump is like every warmongering US president - except for one thing
content: But perhaps the key to understanding why Trump behaves the way he does is because of where he sits. Trump, the former real estate developer, views the world as property. Gaza? A premium property just waiting to be rid of war. Greenland rich in resources that can help America. Venezuela? Oil that can be used by America. Shakespeare said the world is a stage. For Trump, it’s prime real estate.

title: Tina Ahuja reacts to Govinda–Sunita divorce rumours, admits turbulent years
content: Sunita Ahuja’s latest vlog turned into a heartfelt family moment after her daughter Tina Ahuja surprised her on Women’s Day with flowers, gifts and a solitaire ring. The vlog also saw Tina briefly addressing rumours about her parents’ marriage and admitting that the last couple of years have been challenging.

title: 'Want to win more trophies': Suryakumar Yadav's big statement after T20 World Cup glory
content: Suryakumar Yadav, fresh off captaining India to the T20 World Cup title, expressed his elation and reflected on the team's winning streak. He aims to build on this momentum, targeting Olympic gold in 2028 and further ICC trophies, while acknowledging the honor of being compared to legendary captains like Dhoni and Rohit.

title: Army columns deployed in Meghalaya after 2 killed in police firing; curfew clamped
content: Army columns were deployed in Meghalaya's West Garo Hills after two died in security forces' firing during violent clashes over GHADC election nominations. Tensions flared between tribal and non-tribal groups regarding candidate eligibility. A curfew is in effect, and internet services are suspended to quell unrest and restore order.

title: Obama thinks they are real, Trump has 'UFO speech ready': Are we close to finding aliens?
content: The possibility of alien life has moved from fiction to scientific inquiry, fueled by government interest in UAP reports. While scientists haven't confirmed alien spacecraft, the universe's vastness sparks questions about life beyond Earth.  Despite discoveries, the Fermi Paradox—the lack of evidence for alien civilizations—remains a profound cosmic puzzle.

title: 20-year-old finds unclaimed land, declares himself first Gen Z president
content: A 20-year-old, Daniel Jackson, claims to have founded the Free Republic of Verdis on a disputed strip along the Danube River between Croatia and Serbia. Arguing the land is terra nullius, he built an online micronation with citizens and passports. Croatian authorities removed him after a 2023 settlement attempt, leaving him running the project from exile in the United Kingdom.

title: Obesity isn’t just weight: Doctor explains how excess fat harms your heart, joints, metabolism
content: Obesity transcends mere numbers on a scale; it’s a holistic health challenge affecting the heart, joints, and overall metabolism. The silent burden of excess fat can lead to serious complications such as heart disease and sleep apnea. Health professionals advocate that even small changes in weight through smart eating, consistent activity, and stress reduction can yield immense health improvements.

title: US-Iran war: How can India shock-proof itself against future oil, LPG, LNG crisis
content: The Middle East crisis has brought to fore with clarity an important fact - while India says it is comfortably placed on its oil, LPG, and LNG supplies - there is need to step up pace on long-term plans.

title: Now Rs 131 cr: How much has BCCI rewarded India for ICC triumphs?
content: After India’s T20 World Cup 2026 triumph, the BCCI announced a Rs 131 crore reward for the team and support staff. Each of the 15 players is expected to receive Rs 6 crore, with the rest shared among staff. India beat New Zealand by 96 runs in the final, becoming the first team to retain the title and win the tournament three times.

title: TACO vs Tehran: Who will blink first—Trump or Khamenei?
content: On Day 11 of the US-Iran war, President Trump claims devastating military losses and a swift end, while Iran signals prolonged resistance. This conflict is evolving into a test of endurance, with Iran aiming to outlast Washington and Israel through attrition and disruption, while Trump faces domestic pressure from rising oil prices and potential midterm backlash.

title: Lakshya admits self-expectations added to All England 'heartbreak'
content: India’s Lakshya Sen finished runner-up at the 2026 All England Open, losing the final to Taiwan’s Lin Chun-Yi. Fatigue from a gruelling 97-minute semi-final and a foot blister affected his performance. Despite disappointment, Sen reflected positively on the week, highlighting his improved belief and expectations compared to 2022 after defeating several top-ranked opponents.

title: Only for India? Trump's msg to Europe on easing of Russian oil sanctions
content: The United States has informed its European partners that any additional easing of sanctions on Russian crude would largely be confined to shipments destined for India.

title: 'Willing to talk to Iran', says Trump, but warns new leader won't 'live in peace'
content: Trump said that the Iran war was going to "end soon", without providing any specific timeline. He also warned Tehran that if they tried "anything cute", the US would end the nation. He called the Iran war a "short term excursion." During a CBS interview, he said that the war was "very complete" and warned Iran against doing "anything cute".

title: Opposition walks out of Rajya Sabha as debate on Bengal voter roll revision is denied
content: As Opposition parties raise the Special Intensive Revision in West Bengal, Leader of the House and Union Minister J.P. Nadda reminds them there was a detailed debate on electoral reforms

title: Mass suspensions, mics switched off: Opposition talking points in debate on resolution to remove Speaker
content: Opposition MPs also pull up the government over the failure to appoint a Deputy Speaker for last 12 years

title: Mamata ends SIR stir, says game is not yet over
content: During her five-day protest, the West Bengal Chief Minister and her party aides targeted the Election Commission of India; the Commission is working as ‘an agent of the BJP to snatch voting rights away’ from the people of the State, she says

title: Cabinet nod for 23% hike in cost of link road from Noida airport to Delhi-Mumbai Expressway
content: Revised cost of project is ₹3,630 cr; project to be developed on Hybrid Annuity Mode in Haryana, U.P.; 11 km of 31-km link road will be an elevated highway

title: Don’t carry out any work on ground for now on Sharavathi pumped storage hydroelectric project, Karnataka High Court directs State government
content: A Division Bench comprising Chief Justice Vibhu Bakhru and Justice C.M. Poonacha passed the interim order on the petition filed by Akhilesh Chipli of Shivamogga and two others.

title: Dermatologists warn against rise in unqualified cosmetic procedures
content: Indian Association of Dermatologists, Venereologists and Leprologists Karnataka branch released a QR code that allows members of the public to identify qualified dermatologists in Karnataka.

title: SFC recommends 5% of GST revenue allocation to municipal bodies, including GBA corporations
content: The Fifth State Finance Commission says such a measure will help increase the revenue of municipal bodies by 52%

title: BRS to introduce Private Member Bill in Assembly seeking legal backing to six guarantees
content: Working president of BRS KTR says half of the Congress government’s tenure lapsed with most of the promises remaining unkept

title: Six persons honoured with IIT-M’s First Principles Teachers Award 2026
content: The awards were presented to school teachers for their contributions to teaching of physics, chemistry, and math, thereby building strong foundations for future engineers

title: Nurses’ strike continues to affect private hospitals in Kerala
content: Functioning of key departments in some of the major institutions in districts such as Kozhikode has been severely affected and the patients are left in the lurch

title: Form special tribunals to hear appeals of people left out of Bengal SIR, says Supreme Court
content: The Supreme Court asked the Chief Justice of Calcutta High Court to request former Chief Justices and judges of High Courts to preside over the Benches of the appellate tribunals once they are notified by the Election Commission

title: Cabinet approves two rail projects
content: These projects include a fourth rail line between Santragachi – Kharagpur in West Bengal and another connecting Sainthia in West Bengal and Pakur in Jharkhand

title: Nitish outlines five-year vision for Bihar during last leg of Samriddhi Yatra
content: During his public meetings in Supaul and Madhepura district, Kumar did not utter a word about going to Rajya Sabha or leaving the post of Chief Minister

title: As energy scarcity hits Bangladesh, India sends diesel using ‘Friendship Pipeline’
content: There is a request from Bangladesh for more petroleum supplies, but that issue will be addressed after prioritising India’s domestic demand, sources say

title: Biodiversity Study Centre at Kidoor inaugurated
content: The facility, developed as part of the Kumbla rural tourism project under the Kasaragod Development Package, was inaugurated by Manjeswaram MLA A.K.M. Ashraf

title: Ratna Bharat Raitha Samaj to stage protest over problems caused by Benne Halla
content: The samaj will hold the protest in Dharwad on Thursday demanding that the State govt. release ₹3,000 crore to provide a permanent solution

title: Gas from new sources will end shortage, say officials
content: Importing natural gas from U.S., Norway has now become viable, though the shipping distance may prolong shortage of the cooking fuel, they say; domestic LPG production has been increased

title: Three govt. employees terminated for ‘anti-national’ activities in J&K
content: Shaleen Kabra, financial commissioner, Jal Shakti Department, directed ‘disengagement’ from service of three employees

title: Gulf tensions trigger LPG supply concerns in Andhra Pradesh
content: Domestic stocks adequate for 25 to 30 days, say officials as hotels report restrictions on commercial cylinder refills amid shipping uncertainty near Strait of Hormuz

title: Surendran’s possible candidature reportedly sparks differences in BJP’s Manjeswaram unit
content: K. Surendran reportedly steps up activities in the constituency amid indications he may contest from Manjeswaram

title: 'Meat-Eating Priest' Expelled From UP Temple After Viral Photo Sparks Row
content: A priest, who was allegedly mobbed here after a purported photograph of him eating non-vegetarian food went viral, has been expelled from duties at the temple by the 'mahant' -- his father -- while his whereabouts are unknown, officials said.

title: "Absolutely No Shortage Of Fuel": Minister Amid Global Energy Uncertainty
content: Union Minister Piyush Goyal assured that India is not facing any fuel shortage despite the growing uncertainty in global energy markets due to the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

title: Trump Warns Of Severe Military Consequences If Iran Mines Strait Of Hormuz
content: US President Donald Trump warned Iran on Tuesday not to mine the Strait of Hormuz, after Tehran vowed that no Gulf oil would pass through the key waterway while the Middle East war is ongoing.

title: "Aggressor Must Be Punished": Iran Says Not Seeking Ceasefire
content: Iran is not seeking a ceasefire in the war against the United States and Israel, the powerful Iranian parliament speaker said on Tuesday, adding that the "aggressor" should be punished.

title: About 140 US Military Personnel Injured In Iran War: Pentagon
content: About 140 US military personnel have been wounded in attacks since the start of the war against Iran, the Pentagon said on Tuesday.

title: In Khamenei's Will, He Didn't Want Son To Be Iran's Supreme Leader: Report
content: Few people wanted Mojtaba Khamenei to become Iran's next supreme leader, not US President Donald Trump, and not even his own father.

title: Iran-Israel War LIVE Updates: Jaishankar Speaks To Iran's Foreign Minister About Middle East War
content: US-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran launched new attacks Tuesday at Gulf Arab countries as it keeps up pressure on the region, while five pro-Iranian militants were killed by an airstrike in northern Iraq.

title: Rajasthan To Drop 2-Child Norm, But Only For Candidates In Local Bodies Polls
content: The Rajasthan government has done away with the two-child norm for candidates in panchayat and municipal elections in the state - a law introduced by the Bhairon Singh Shekhawat government in 1995 to control the state's population.

title: In A First, J&K Government Sacks 3 Jal Shakti Workers Over Terror Links
content: In a first such action by the Jammu and Kashmir government, three daily-wage workers in the Jal Shakti department have been sacked over alleged terror links.

title: India Sourcing Oil From 40 Nations By Alternate Routes Amid Hormuz Blockade
content: The Strait of Hormuz blocked by Iran amid its war with the US and Israel, India has started sourcing its crude oil from 40 countries.

title: "Go Sell Sweaters In Ludhiana": Chief Justice To Trader Over Frivolous Petition In Supreme Court
content: The Supreme Court on Tuesday saw Chief Justice of India Surya Kant losing his cool at a trader from Punjab's Ludhiana who filed a public interest litigation (PIL) seeking inclusion of the PM Cares Fund under the RTI Act.

title: Bhagwant Mann Flags Risk To Farmers From India-US Trade Deal
content: Bhagwant Mann on Tuesday cautioned that the interim India-US trade agreement would open India's agricultural markets to heavily subsidised American produce, which would leave farmers in Punjab and other parts of the country struggling to compete.

title: Lightning Or New Missile? Blue Flares Light Up Iran Skies Amid Strikes
content: The night sky over Karaj, west of Iran's Tehran, turned bright blue, mirroring lightning, reportedly after US and Israeli missiles struck energy infrastructure in the area early Tuesday.

title: Mumbai Dentist Dies By Suicide, Boyfriend Arrested For Abetment
content: A 24-year-old student of dentistry has died by suicide at her Mumbai home, allegedly due to mental harassment by her boyfriend.

title: US' "Tactical Error" Ahead Of Iran War As It Dismissed Ukraine's Plan
content: Months before the Iran conflict escalated, Kyiv offered Washington battle-tested technology to counter Iranian drones. The proposal was dismissed, a decision some US officials now call a major "tactical error", according to a report by Axios.

title: 40 Lakh Women Get Rs 9,000 Each In Assam Under Arunodoi Scheme
content: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma virtually distributed the benefits to lakhs of beneficiaries at the Jyoti Bishnu Antarjatik Kala Mandir auditorium in Khanapara, Guwahati.

title: Why The War In Middle East Is Triggering LPG Prices In India
content: As chaos intensifies in West Asia, India is feeling the heat at home. Liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) - the cooking fuel for more than 33 crore households and the backbone of restaurant kitchens - has suddenly become hard to get.

title: Air India Fares To Rise As It Imposes Fuel Surcharge Amid Iran War
content: Air India will introduce a phased increase in fuel surcharges across its domestic and international network, the airline announced today, citing a sharp rise in jet fuel prices driven by the Iran-US war in the Middle East.

title: Indian Forum In UAE Sets Up 24x7 Control Room For Stranded Indians
content: Amid the ongoing travel disruptions in the Middle East, the Indian People's Forum (IPF) has set up a 24x7 multipurpose control room in the UAE to assist stranded Indians.

title: Opinion: Opinion |  This Poll Season, Mamata Banerjee's Hindu Outreach Goes The Extra Mile
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has been on an overdrive for about a year, to announce and inaugurate temples in various corners of the state, amid BJP allegations that she is "appeasing" the minority  community.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Real Madrid monitoring Man City's Rodri
content: real madrid have been linked with a move to atletico madrid . the 29-year-old is expected to make a decision on his future at the Etihad . arsenal and Barcelona are interested in borussia Dortmund 's Julian Brandt .

title: Air tickets to get costlier as Air India to levy fuel surcharge due to hike in ATF prices
content: e-Paper The View From India Looking at World Affairs from the Indian perspective . first day first show news and reviews from the world of cinema and streaming . the weekly newsletter from science writers takes the jargon out of science and puts the fun in!

title: The stats behind Spurs' 17th-minute subbing out of GK Kinsky vs. Atlético
content: Antonn Kinsky was substituted just 17 minutes into the round-of-16 tie . the 22-year-old had been handed his first appearance since a 2-0 defeat to atlético . he had been substituted in the UEFA champions league knockout stages . his substitute ties the earliest a goalkeeper has ever been replaced .

title: Playoff watch: Who are winning the West wild cards?
content: the blackhawks are in the first wild-card spot, with 73 points through 64 games . the mammoth have gone 4-2-1 since the Olympic break . they face off against the wild ( 8 p.m. ET, ESPN+/Hulu ).

title: MMA divisional rankings: Borralho bounces back, lands in middleweight top 5
content: caio Borralho has landed in a higher position in ESPN's divisional MMA rankings . he defeated Reinier de Ridder by unanimous decision in the co-main event of UFC 326 in Las Vegas . the victory boosted him up two spots to no. 5 in the 185-pound ranking . it was a second straight loss, but he remains at no. 9.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts and rumors
content:
Adam Schefter tells Pat McAfee that Romeo Doubs is heading to the Patriots and explains what that now means for A.J. Brown's chances of heading to New England. (1:41) NFL free agency  officially begins at 4 p.m. ET on Wednesday, with the start of the new league year. But as of Monday at noon ET, when the legal negotiation window opened, teams can agree to terms with players. Quarterback  Malik Willis  is signing with the Dolphins and  Tua Tagovailoa  is signing with the Falcons, but which team could be interested in  Kyler Murray ? What's the latest on edge rusher  Trey Hendrickson ? And which big-name players are available as trade candidates? We're tracking all the action of 2026 NFL free agency. Follow along live here with the latest signings, trades, cuts and contract restructures. 2026 NFL free agency coverage: 
 Top 100 rankings  |  Grading big moves 
 Winners, losers  |  Mini mock draft

title: Army called out, Net blocked after 2 killed in violence in run-up to Meghalaya council polls
content: Prabin Kalita is a journalist at The Times of India and is currently the Chief of Bureau (northeast). He has been reporting in mainstream Indian national media since 2001. He has been a field journalist reporting gamut of issues from India’s northeastern region and major developments in neighbouring countries like Myanmar, China, Bhutan and Bangladesh concerning India and northeastern region. He has been covering insurgency—internal and cross-border, politics, natural calamities, environment etc. He is a post-graduate in Geological Sciences from Gauhati University. Read More

title: How AI may have handed Iran’s proxies a map to US infra
content: Chethan Kumar is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Times of India. Aside from specialising in Space & Science, he has reported extensively on varied topics, with special focus on defence, policy and data stories. He has covered multiple elections, too. As a young democracy grows out of adolescence, Chethan feels, there are reels of tales emerging which need to be captured. To do this, he alternates between the mundane goings-on of the Common Man and the wonder-filled worlds of scientists and scamsters, politicians and soldiers. In a career spanning nearly 18 years, he has reported from multiple datelines — Houston, Florida, Kochi, Hyderabad, Chennai, Sriharikota (AP), NH-1 (J&K Highway), New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Raichur, Bhatkal, Mysuru, Chamarajanagar, to name a few —  but is based out of Bengaluru, India’s science capital that also hosts the ISRO HQ. Read More

title: In crunch amid West Asia crisis, govt says domestic PNG, CNG, LPG production priority

Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. title: NCERT apologises for 'judicial graft' chapter, pulls out Class VIII book
content: the book - "Exploring Society: India and Beyond" Grade 8 (Part 2) - contained Chapter 4 titled "The Role of Judiciary in our Society" the chapter had described corruption, massive case backlog and shortage of judges as challenges faced by the judiciary .

title: UCC key to end gender bias in laws: SC
content: suprem court says UCC is most effective tool to remove discrimination against women in marriage, succession, inheritance and property rights under Muslim personal law and other customary laws . advocate says inferior inheritance rights given to women under the Muslim Personal Law application Act, 1937 violated their right to equality and non-discrimination .

title: Flagging dangers of social media, SC to hear Centre plea on fact-check units
content: a HC judgment struck down amendments to the Information and Technology Rules that allowed Centre to set up fact-check units to flag fake social media content . the govt already is empowered to issue take down orders and a social media platform or intermediary is bound to remove or take down such content within 48 hours of receiving the notice .

title: El Nino may hit India's monsoon, hotter months ahead, says WMO
content: Vishwa Mohan is Senior Editor at The Times of India. He writes on environment, climate change, agriculture, water resources and clean energy, tracking policy issues and climate diplomacy. He has been covering Parliament since 2003 to see how politics shaped up domestic policy and India’s position at global platform. Before switching over to explore sustainable development issues, Vishwa had covered internal security and investigative agencies for more than a decade. Read More

title: Nod to changes to widen transgender definition
content: cabinet approves proposal to amend the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019 . it seeks to make the law more "inclusive" so that all bona fide persons receive benefits and protection . there has been debate over the implementation of the law, with community leaders raising concerns .

title: Indian shops, restaurants in UK's Wembley vandalised in targeted attacks
content: manek Chowk was trashed by a gang in wembley, london, last week . gang allegedly terrorised shopkeepers and restaurant owners in a targeted attack . this follows the attack in Harrow last week, wherein gang attacked Holi revellers .
